meta.originator: Commandant of the Marine Corps — M&RA (MPO)
meta.signer: David A. Ottignon, Deputy Commandant for Manpower and Reserve Affairs
meta.subject: Marine Corps Policy Concerning Parenthood and Pregnancy

content.p: To transmit changes to the basic Order, because the postpartum exemption period for physical fitness testing and body composition standards has been expanded from nine months to 12 months.
This change illustrates Commandant of the Marine Corps directed updates to this Order.
Page 4, paragraph 4.b.(8)(l), change "nine" to "12" and change "nine" to "12". This change will expand the post-partum exemption period for return to weight standards.

Page 6, paragraph 4.b.(12)(f), change "nine" to "12" and change "nine-month" to "12 month". This change expands the post-partum exemption period for complying with physical fitness and body composition standards.

Page 6, paragraph 4.b.(13), change "nine" to "12" and change "nine-month" to "12 month". This change expands the post-partum exemption period for complying with physical fitness and body composition standards.

Page 2-3, paragraph 3.b.(7), change "nine" to "12" and change "nine-month" to "12 month". This change expands the post-partum exemption period for complying with physical fitness and body composition standards.
Page 2-4, paragraph 4.d., change "nine" to "12" and change "nine" to "12". This change expands the post-partum exemption period for assignment to the body composition or military appearance programs.

Page 2-8, paragraph 7.a.(1), change "nine" to "12". This change expands the post-partum exemption period for unit physical training, Physical Fitness Test (PFT), Combat Fitness Test (CFT), and body composition and military appearance programs.
Page 2-8, paragraph 7.a.(1)(d), change "nine" to "12". This change expands the post-partum exemption period for physical fitness.
Page A-2, paragraph 3., change "nine" to "12". This change expands the post-partum exemption period for PFT, CFT, and acceptable weight standards.
Page D-2, paragraph 25., change "nine" to "12". This change expands the post-partum exemption period for PFT, CFT, and acceptable weight standards.
Submit all recommendations concerning this Order to Manpower and Reserve Affairs (M&RA), Manpower Military Policy (MPO) Branch.

Records created as a result of this directive shall be managed according to National Archives and Records Administration (NARA)-approved dispositions per SECNAV M-5210.1 CH-1 to ensure proper maintenance, use, accessibility and preservation, regardless of format or medium. Refer to MCO 5210.11F for Marine Corps records management policy and procedures.

Any misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Personally Identifiable Information (PII) may result in both civil and criminal penalties. All collection, use, maintenance, or dissemination of PII shall be in accordance with the Privacy Act of 1974, as amended (5 U.S.C. 552a) and implemented per SECNAVINST 5211.5F.
This Order is applicable to the Marine Corps Total Force.
This Order is effective the date signed.

paragraph.heading: Enclosure (2) — Marine Corps Policy Concerning Parenthood and Pregnancy
